description:
OL 316 (REV. 7/2020) WWW
Autonomous Mode 
Conventional Mode
Additional information attached.
On May 17, 2024 at 11:45 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision 
involving a passenger car on Interstate 280 North between exits 54B and 55.  
 
The Waymo AV was traveling north on Interstate 280 in the rightmost lane in autonomous mode with a test driver seated in the driver's 
seated position when it approached a slow-moving heavy truck equipped with a truck-mounted light matrix displaying a yellow arrow 
indicating that traffic should merge to the left. The Waymo AV slowed down behind the heavy truck and the test driver transitioned the 
Waymo AV to manual mode. The test driver stopped the Waymo AV and activated the left turn signal. Several vehicles passed the Waymo 
AV on the left side before the test driver manually initiated a left lane change, and the front passenger side of a passenger car which was 
passing the Waymo AV on the left made contact with the front driver side of the Waymo AV. At the time of the impact, the Waymo AV's 
Level 4 ADS was not engaged and a test driver was operating the Waymo AV in manual mode.  Both vehicles sustained damage.
Print
Clear Form
